reason: Epic, surreal odyssey that dissolves time and reality with stunning visuals
Kafkaesque dystopia where logic fractures in a richly grotesque, dreamlike world
Blade Runner’s neon-noir future blurs memories and machines in haunting visuals
Vertigo’s dizzying dream–reality shifts wrapped in Hitchcockian beauty
Lost Highway’s nonlinear, nightmarish mood leaves you unmoored and mesmerized
Pink Floyd – The Wall’s abstract imagery and haunting surrealism unsettle profoundly
The Shining’s eerie, shifting hotel corridors erode sanity in a beautiful nightmare
A Clockwork Orange’s stylized brutality and warped visuals feel chillingly unreal
Fantasia’s hallucinatory animation turns music into strange, awe-tinged visions
Delicatessen’s dark, surreal comedy mixes whimsy and unease in a bizarre setting
